input.content:
You will be given a graph as an adjacency list, an operation, and a node. Your answer must be the set of all reachable nodes for the operation. Return the answer in the last line of your response in the form 'Final Answer: [node1, node2, ...]'.

Adjacency list:
node_0 -> node_1, node_2
node_1 -> node_3
node_2 -> node_3, node_4
node_3 -> node_5
node_4 -> node_5

Operation: Find the parents of node node_3.

You will be given a graph as an adjacency list, an operation, and a node. Your answer must be the set of all reachable nodes for the operation. Return the answer in the last line of your response in the form 'Final Answer: [node1, node2, ...]'.

Adjacency list:
alpha -> beta, gamma
beta -> delta
gamma -> delta, epsilon
delta -> zeta
epsilon -> zeta

Operation: Find the parents of node zeta.

You will be given a graph as an adjacency list, an operation, and a node. Your answer must be the set of all reachable nodes for the operation. Return the answer in the last line of your response in the form 'Final Answer: [node1, node2, ...]'.

Adjacency list:
node_0 -> node_1, node_2
node_1 -> node_3, node_4
node_2 -> node_5
node_3 -> node_6
node_4 -> node_6
node_5 -> node_7

Operation: Perform a BFS from node node_0 and return only the nodes at exactly depth 2 (not nodes at intermediate depths).

You will be given a graph as an adjacency list, an operation, and a node. Your answer must be the set of all reachable nodes for the operation. Return the answer in the last line of your response in the form 'Final Answer: [node1, node2, ...]'.

Adjacency list:
root -> a, b
a -> c, d
b -> e
c -> f
d -> f, g
e -> g
f -> h
g -> h

Operation: Perform a BFS from node root and return only the nodes at exactly depth 3 (not nodes at intermediate depths).

You will be given a graph as an adjacency list, an operation, and a node. Your answer must be the set of all reachable nodes for the operation. Return the answer in the last line of your response in the form 'Final Answer: [node1, node2, ...]'.

Adjacency list:
n0 -> n1
n1 -> n2, n3
n2 -> n4
n3 -> n4, n5
n4 -> n6
n5 -> n6
n6 -> n7

Operation: Find the parents of node n4.
